entities.name: Jannes and Jambres
entities.description: False prophets mentioned in relation to miracles and plagues (serpents and frogs)

entities.name: Pharaos magicians
entities.description: Magicians of Pharaoh compared to witches in terms of cunning and juggling knacks

entities.name: Erasmus
entities.description: Author of a story regarding two Alcumysts and the phenomena of longation and curtation

entities.name: Bealphares
entities.description: A spirit that can be bound or lost, mentioned in an experiment

entities.name: Paymon
entities.description: One of three spirits that can be raised with wonderful things effected through their assistance

entities.name: Bathin
entities.description: One of three spirits that can be raised with wonderful things effected through their assistance

entities.name: Barma
entities.description: One of three spirits that can be raised with wonderful things effected through their assistance

entities.name: Luridan
entities.description: A spirit that can be made a familiar; also associated with Belelah

entities.name: Belelah
entities.description: Another name for the familiar Luridan

entities.name: Balkin
entities.description: The Master of Luridan

entities.name: John Baptist
entities.description: Subject of a juggling trick called 'the decollation of John Baptist'

entities.name: S. Margaret
entities.description: Subject of a story involving fighting a real devil and a miracle wrought by a priest

entities.name: S. Lucie
entities.description: Subject of a strange story following a miracle of S. Margaret

entities.name: Cornelius Agrippa
entities.description: Author whose writings on magic are mentioned in relation to Nero

entities.name: Carolus Gallus
entities.description: Author whose writings on magic are mentioned in relation to Nero

entities.name: James Sprenger
entities.description: Inquisitor and compiler of Malleus Maleficarum

entities.name: Henry Institor
entities.description: Inquisitor and compiler of Malleus Maleficarum

entities.name: Psellus
entities.description: Philosopher whose opinions on spirits are discussed and disproved

entities.name: Plato
entities.description: Philosopher whose opinions on spirits, devils, and angels are discussed

attributes.contribution: nine orders of spirits and angels

entities.name: Socrates
entities.description: Philosopher mentioned in relation to having a familiar devil

entities.name: John Calvin
entities.description: Theologian whose opinion on the devil is mentioned

entities.name: Alcumystrie
entities.description: The art of alchemy/alchemists, involving words and devices to deceive eyes

entities.name: Conjuration
entities.description: The act of calling or binding spirits, often discussed in a critical context

entities.name: Juggling knacks
entities.description: Deceptive tricks or sleight of hand used to simulate magic

entities.name: Theurgy
entities.description: A form of magic or divine work, discussed with a confutation

entities.name: Magical Circles
entities.description: Instruments used for the institution of magic and raising spirits

entities.name: Witchcraft
entities.description: The practice of using supernatural powers, which the author seeks to define and disprove

entities.name: Alchemical stones and waters
entities.description: Substances associated with the art of Alcumysterie

entities.name: Christall stone
entities.description: A medium used to inclose a spirit

entities.name: Holy water
entities.description: Substance used in religious and exorcism rituals

summary: This passage is a table of contents and excerpt from Reginald Scot's 'Discoverie of Witchcraft', detailing various 'juggling knacks' (sleight of hand), alchemical deceptions, and the practices of conjuration. The text systematically lists and then provides critical refutations for various magical claims, including the binding of spirits like Bealphares and the existence of demonic powers, ultimately arguing that witchcraft is a form of deception or 'cousenage'.
